metric_value: Data and telecom systems still separate in most organizations
notes: Aberdeen research conclusion; IT has historically had little to do with telephony decisions

metric_value: Enterprise users investigating migration to ATM and SONET for high-speed on-demand multimedia LAN/WAN
notes: Aberdeen research on enterprise network planning; consolidation of LAN/WAN traffic driving demand

metric_value: Up to 192 voice ports per peripheral node
notes: LIGHT Peripheral Node capacity; scalable by adding nodes

metric_value: OC-3 fiber optic (155 Mbps) backbone integrating voice and LAN packet data; ATM cells for voice+data multiplexing
notes: Madge MultiNet hub provides 1.28 Gbps internal LANswitch bus; ATM module converts Ethernet to ATM cells

metric_value: T1/E1 DSU links and PRI gateways via SX-2000 LIGHT DSU nodes
notes: WAN connectivity via LIGHT DSU or Network Gateway nodes; supports analog trunk access

metric_value: Ethernet, Token Ring, FDDI, and 100 Base-T in switched or shared media modes
notes: Token Ring support differentiates from Ethernet-only competitors; broader enterprise compatibility

metric_value: Managed from UNIX workstation running HP OpenView; unified management interface for end-to-end control
notes: Single management interface consolidates voice and data network management

metric_value: Evolution: proprietary PBX → modular distributed nodes → open-standards call server on broadband backbone
notes: 3-phase migration: (1) modular PBX breakup, (2) peripheral/gateway nodes, (3) network management layer

metric_value: Open standards + Token Ring + Ethernet support + strategic IT partnerships (DEC, Intel, Microsoft, Madge)
notes: Token Ring support cited as significant differentiator; first major PBX manufacturer for full enterprise CTI

metric_value: Eliminates wiring redundancies by combining voice and data on single OC-3 backbone
notes: Primary cost benefit: eliminating separate voice and data cabling

metric_value: Adding ports does not affect existing ones (Madge Networks LAN switching characteristic)
notes: Key enterprise requirement: non-disruptive capacity expansion

metric_name: NeVaDa benefit 3: Unified multimedia desktop access
metric_value: Enables CTI multimedia applications at desktop cost-effectively via single IT-centric infrastructure
notes: Aberdeen identifies this as main long-term benefit; not fully realized in 1996 due to ATM evolution gap

metric_value: Initial distrust of telecom vendors in IT community; perception of lacking integration/software skills
notes: Critical barrier: IT community concerned about reliability of telecom functions in data environment

metric_value: Must secure ATM technology to meet proposed ATM-enabled upgrade in timely fashion
notes: NeVaDa currently OC-3 fiber; full ATM upgrade required for future roadmap

metric_value: CTI arena becoming increasingly competitive; must implement vision quickly and effectively
notes: Aberdeen warning: competitive threat from multiple converging vendors; time-to-market critical

metric_value: IT view of the world is and will continue to be the de facto communications model; voice must integrate into IT
notes: Aberdeen agrees with Mitel's IT-centric vision; telecom companies must adapt to IT standards/culture

metric_value: Logical and required step; as migration-path strategy makes good common sense; first major PBX manufacturer for full enterprise CTI
notes: Conditional endorsement; success requires ATM execution and IT credibility building

metric_value: True convergence of voice, data, and video in single IT-centric infrastructure will occur
notes: Both Aberdeen and Mitel affirm; Mitel's vision first articulated in 1991

metric_value: Mitel can achieve CTI market leadership if it implements vision quickly and secures ATM technology
notes: Conditional prediction; depends on execution speed and partnership expansion (Teleos/video components)

metric_value: VERIFIED — VoIP (SIP/H.323) fully replaced PBX by ~2010; UCaaS (Teams, Zoom, Webex) fulfills unified multimedia vision
notes: IT-centric model (SIP over IP) prevailed exactly as Aberdeen predicted; traditional PBX became legacy

metric_value: Survived via multiple restructurings; became significant UC vendor; acquired Aastra (2013), ShoreTel (2017); filed Ch.11 Mar 2025, exited Jun 2025
notes: Mitel never achieved 'global CTI leadership' but survived and became mid-market UC leader. Source: Wikipedia Mitel; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitel

metric_value: Filed bankruptcy April 2003; operational business acquired by Network Technology/Ringdale 2006
notes: Token Ring market declined rapidly after 1998; Madge's core business evaporated. Source: Wikipedia Madge Networks; Computerworld 2008
